channel.title: News on Strichliste ‒ The Tally Sheet Replacement for Your Organization
channel.description: Recent content in News on Strichliste ‒ The Tally Sheet Replacement for Your Organization

item.title: strichliste 3 is here
item.description: strichliste 3 is one single application with the look and feel long-time users know. One thing to install, one thing to update — with a lot of care for the screen by the fridge.

item.title: Release v1.6.1
item.description:
We’re proud to announce the new strichliste release v1.61 with a lot of fixes and some new features:

  • Charge account by PayPal
  • Visual fixes (contrast, colors, …)
  • More input validation
  • ! Extracted strichliste config to separate strichliste.yaml file !

To use the PayPal-Feature, you have to configure your recipient address in the strichliste-server strichliste.yaml. To complete a PayPal-transaction it’s mandatory to press “return to merchant” at the end of the paypal transaction. We’re currently investigating ways to do that automatically. PayPal’s own rm=1 parameter does not seem to work here.

item.title: Builds
item.description:
Our build infrastructure and repo names have changed!

If you want a build with frontend and backend included, just head to strichliste/releases. The front-end and it’s builds are available at strichliste-web-frontend and the backend is located at strichliste-backend

item.title: LDAP Importer
item.description:
Strichliste just got a new, experimental command! You can now import users from a LDAP source. See the documentation here at our Commands.md for more details!

You can also let this run as a cronjob to update the users mailaddress or create new ones as they appear in your LDAP directory.

Feedback is appreciated!

item.title: Strichliste 2 released!
item.description:
After a lot of concept work, strichliste 2 has now a stable API and also a well designed front-end. We’ve also changed our tech stack completely. Instead of node.js in the backend, and angular js in the front-end, we’re now using a symfony based PHP backend providing the API and also command-line tools for several tasks.

The new frontend is completely responsive and uses react and redux as it’s core components.

item.title: More features to come
item.description: Stay tuned for more features! We’re now working on some wanted features in the next strichliste release. These features are: money transfer, splitting bills, cancellation of the last order within a specific time range and we’re preparing the database for articles

item.title: Hello World
item.description: strichliste has it’s own homepage now! We’re in the process of getting the content ready, stay tuned for updates.
